creator: pavel.plesov@gmail.com
text:
Hello,

Thanks for the hints! Support of NOPORTDOCS is included.

Now portlint -C produces the following output for me:

[root /usr/ports/x11/xmobar]# portlint -C
WARN: Makefile: [60]: possible direct use of command "install" found.
use ${INSTALL_foobaa} instead.
WARN: no CVS directories. Use -N to check a new port.
0 fatal errors and 2 warnings found.

On Tue, Mar 10, 2009 at 3:46 PM, Gabor PALI <pgj@freebsd.org> wrote:
> Hi,
>
> I usually use the -C flag (i.e. "portlint -C", aka. "Pedantic committer
> flag" :)). =A0It equals to "-abct", and I think flag "-t" spits these
> errors and warnings.
>
> FreeBSD Porter's Handbook says [1]:
>
> ``Do check your work with portlint before you submit or commit it.
> portlint warns you about many common errors, both functional and
> stylistic. For a new (or repocopied) port, portlint -A is the most
> thorough; for an existing port, portlint -C is sufficient.''
>
>
> Of course, if you invoke portlint by -C, you will see some other
> warnings (like the missing PORTDOCS). =A0Well, they are not your faults,
> however, adding support for (NO)PORTDOCS to x11/xmobar would be a good id=
ea.
>
> I recommend you to fix up these errors and warnings, check the port by
> `portlint -C`, and re-submit your patch. =A0Please, note that I have
> already tested it in Tinderbox, and it builds with no problem, but I
> think these whitespace issues should be addressed.
>
> (For objectivity, some elder committers are CCed. =A0Of course, they are
> free to override my recommendations.)
>
>
> Cheers,
> :g
>
> [1]
> http://www.freebsd.org/doc/en/books/porters-handbook/testing-portlint.htm=
l
>


--=20
Pavel Plesov
